title: I AM LIMITLESS Conference 2020
description: Jointly established by New York University and East China Normal University, NYU Shanghai is the third degree-granting campus in NYU’s global network, joining NYU Abu Dhabi and NYU's campus in New York City.
text:
I AM LIMITLESS Conference 2020
Introduction of the conference
The I AM LIMITLESS Conference is an annual conference held by the Center for Career Development. This year, the conference theme was “Social Impact & innovation: a Force for Good,” and was held from April 11 - April 17, 2020. During the event, more than 400 students, and 20 alumni and professionals working at NGOs, international organizations, social enterprises, and social media came together in online sessions, where they discussed topics such as poverty, healthcare, environment, and global collaboration. Students gained insight into making an impact on society by listening to the panelists share their career journeys, which included internships, full time jobs and volunteering.
Videos of the Talks and Panels are archived on this page so that students can continue to benefit and gain insight from them.
Trailer
Part A: I AM LIMITLESS Talks
April 11, 9:30 - 11:30 AM
Empowering communities by creating meaningful online connections
Fan Zhang, Director, CSR and sustainability, Kuaishou Online social platforms like Kuaishou are making connections more accessible for all. Online communities are tightly bonded by shared values and mutual trust, providing a new model of self-empowerment. In this session, Fan Zhang shared how these meaningful online connections can break social gaps and uplift communities.
Train Your Curiosity
Yue Su, Chief Editor for Health and Wellness, Hunziyue Today, the public is becoming increasingly open and curious about new developments and technologies. However, we have also encountered bottlenecks in our development. Yue Su says one of the biggest challenges her team faced was when they realized that many of them were beginning to criticize and even feel aversion to change. In this session, Yue shared how her team realized the problem and overcame it.
Re-Make @ NYU Shanghai
Marcela Godoy, Clinical Instructor of Arts, NYU Shanghai Professor Marcela Godoy, who teaches the Re-Made in China course, talked about NYU Shanghai’s Re-Makerspace, an open space for the community to collect and repurpose trash from the campus building, and engage with the greater Shanghai community on environmental issues.
How to Effectively Activate and Leverage Your Social Impact?
Angelina Ye, Manager, APMEN In this session, Angelina Ye offered personal experiences and practical strategies for broadening social impact. She also spoke about achieving results that are greater than the sum of their parts,and finding inspiration.
Part B: I AM LIMITLESS Panel
April 13, Monday, 8:00 - 9:00 PMOffice for Community Engaged LearningThis session is designed to help students begin to reflect upon how their service and volunteer experiences have and will continue to impact their lifelong learning.
Global Awards Spotlight: Conversation on Social Impact
April 14, Tuesday, 8:00 - 9:00 PMGlobal AwardsDirector of Global Awards, Dr. Anna Kendrick, an alumna of the Gates Cambridge Scholarship, moderated this panel with several distinguished guests who have completed scholarship/fellowship programs. The panelists shared their social impact stories, and what led them to these global award programs.
Get it Started: Experience Sharing and New Possibilities in Volunteering
April 15, Wednesday, 8:00 - 9:00 PMOffice for Student InvolvementHighlights: Students participating in DSC service programs shared their experiences and reflections in community service. You can also learn more about the new DSC service student leader program.
Experiences at International Organizations: Career Pathways and Meaning Making
April 16, Thursday, 8:00 - 9:00 PMHongxia Liu, Representative and Director for International Organizations at NYU ShanghaiThis session introduced the world of international organizations (IOs) - including insight on what life is like inside IOs. Watch this interactive panel with our NYU Shanghai’s Representative to International Organizations to learn how to build experiences towards a career in IOs.
Challenges and Opportunities in Building Experience in the Nonprofit Sector
April 17, Friday, 8:00 - 9:00 PMCareer Development CenterStudents who have interned at nonprofits shared their job searching strategies, skill building, and career planning in nonprofits. Watch this panel to learn about ways to make an impact as a college student and resources available to NYU Shanghai students to help you achieve these goals.
Part C: Organization Info Session and Opportunity Fair
NPI Info Session
Opportunity Fair - Link to Handshake
Relevant Resources
Recap Video
Over 300 Students Around the World Attend First-Ever Virtual I Am Limitless ConferenceHighlights of I AM LIMITLESS Talks - Fan Zhang
